title: Episode 2 — Iran War Justification
description: The Foundry Council debates whether the United States was justified in joining Israel's preventive war on Iran. Mercer argues For (a regime days from breakout, with the inspectors blind, cannot be allowed the courtesy of legal patience). Tanaka argues Against (five of the six tests of a just war failed on the documentary record — preventive is not preemption). Kurogane is the Swing — and finds his floor in a hotel in Muscat, where an American diplomat was still talking when the bombs fell.
summary: Was the United States justified in joining Israel's preventive war on Iran? Tanaka, Kurogane, and Mercer debate jus ad bellum, the chronology of the Muscat track, and the question of imminence-by-capability. Juliet moderates.
